content:
## Root Cause

The error comes from `rustls-native-certs` inside `reqwest` (used by `google-cloud-gax`). When your binary — especially a statically-linked musl build — runs on a system without a system CA store, `rustls` fails to load any trust anchors and refuses to open TLS connections.

```
No CA certificates were loaded from the system
```

## Fix 1 — Bundle WebPKI roots into the binary (recommended)

Cargo unifies features across the entire dependency graph. Adding `rustls-tls-webpki-roots` to your top-level `reqwest` entry causes Mozilla's curated CA bundle (`webpki-roots`) to be compiled into the same `reqwest` that `google-cloud-gax` uses, so the binary carries its own trust anchors and never needs to find them on disk.

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
reqwest = { version = "0.13", features = ["json", "rustls-tls-webpki-roots"] }
```

When both `rustls-tls-native-roots` (pulled in by `google-cloud-gax`) **and** `rustls-tls-webpki-roots` are active, `reqwest` merges both root stores, so the binary still honours local corporate CAs if they exist.

---

## Fix 2 — Install system CA certs on the target host (user-side workaround)

If you cannot rebuild, installing the `ca-certificates` package is enough:

```bash
# Debian / Ubuntu
sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates

# Alpine Linux
apk add --no-cache ca-certificates

# Or point reqwest at an explicit bundle
export SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
```

---

## Fix 3 — Install CA certs during the musl build in CI

Even with the Cargo fix above it is good hygiene to have certs present in the build environment for integration tests or `cargo publish`:

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yaml  — inside the build job steps
      - name: Install musl-tools (x86_64-musl)
        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools ca-certificates   # ← add ca-certificates
```

---

## Why musl static binaries are especially affected

| Build type | CA certificate source |
|---|---|
| `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` | Links against glibc, which reads `/etc/ssl/certs` at runtime |
| `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` | No dynamic libc; `rustls-native-certs` has nothing to parse → **zero roots loaded → TLS fails** |

The `webpki-roots` fix is the only approach that makes the binary self-contained and works identically on Alpine, scratch containers, or any host without a CA bundle installed.
I'm going for fix 3, but would it still work in termux

## Fix 3 Does Not Help in Termux

Fix 3 only installs `ca-certificates` inside the **GitHub Actions runner** (the build machine). It has zero effect on where the compiled binary eventually runs. Nothing gets embedded into the binary itself.

### Why Termux Specifically Breaks

`rustls-native-certs` on Linux probes a hardcoded list of standard paths:

```
/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt   ← Debian/Ubuntu
/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt     ← RHEL/Fedora
/usr/lib/ssl/certs/                  ← etc.
```

Termux stores its CA bundle under its own prefix:

```
/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/etc/tls/cert.pem
```

None of the hardcoded paths exist on Android, so `rustls-native-certs` loads **zero roots** and you get the same error.

---

## You Need Fix 1 for Termux to Work

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
reqwest = { version = "0.13", features = ["json", "rustls-tls-webpki-roots"] }
```

This compiles Mozilla's CA bundle directly into the binary. It needs no files on disk at runtime, so it works on:

| Environment | Fix 3 | Fix 1 |
|---|---|---|
| Standard Linux (glibc) | ✅ | ✅ |
| Alpine / musl container | ❌ | ✅ |
| Termux (Android) | ❌ | ✅ |
| Scratch container | ❌ | ✅ |

---

## Keep Fix 3 Anyway

Fix 3 is still worth keeping — it ensures the CI runner itself can reach `crates.io` and other HTTPS endpoints during `cargo build`/`cargo publish` — but pair it with Fix 1 so the **resulting binary** is also self-contained:

```yaml
- name: Install musl-tools (x86_64-musl)
  if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
  run: |
    sudo apt-get update
    sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools ca-certificates
```

```toml
reqwest = { version = "0.13", features = ["json", "rustls-tls-webpki-roots"] }
```

```bash
$ cargo clippy
error: failed to select a version for `reqwest`.
    ... required by package `gce-vm v1.0.4 (/home/ubuntu/workspace/gvm)`
versions that meet the requirements `^0.13` (locked to 0.13.2) are: 0.13.2

package `gce-vm` depends on `reqwest` with feature `rustls-tls-webpki-roots` but `reqwest` does not have that feature.
 available features: __native-tls, __native-tls-alpn, __rustls, __rustls-aws-lc-rs, __tls, blocking, brotli, charset, cookies, default, default-tls, deflate, form, gzip, hickory-dns, http2, http3, json, multipart, native-tls, native-tls-no-alpn, native-tls-vendored, native-tls-vendored-no-alpn, query, rustls, rustls-no-provider, socks, stream, system-proxy, zstd


failed to select a version for `reqwest` which could resolve this conflict
```

## Feature Was Renamed in reqwest 0.13

reqwest 0.13 reorganised its TLS feature flags. The old `rustls-tls-webpki-roots` name from 0.12 no longer exists. Looking at the available features in your error output, the correct one is simply `rustls`:

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
reqwest = { version = "0.13", features = ["json", "rustls"] }
```

### What Each Relevant Feature Does in 0.13

| Feature | Crypto backend | CA roots |
|---|---|---|
| `rustls` | aws-lc-rs | WebPKI (Mozilla bundle, compiled in) |
| `rustls-no-provider` | none — you supply your own | — |
| `default-tls` | native-tls | system store |

`rustls` is exactly what you want — it bundles Mozilla's CA roots into the binary (same end result as the old `rustls-tls-webpki-roots`) and works in Termux or any other environment without a system CA store.

~ $ echo $SSL_CERT_FILE
/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/etc/tls/cert.pem
~ $ gvm status
Error: Error { kind: Exhausted, source: Some(Error { kind: Connect, source: Some(reqwest::Error { kind: Request, url: "https://compute.googleapis.com//compute/v1/projects/european-spallation-source/aggregated/instances?pageToken=&%24alt=json", source: hyper_util::client::legacy::Error(Connect, ConnectError("dns error", Custom { kind: Uncategorized, error: "failed to lookup address information: Try again" })) }) }) }

## TLS Is Fixed — This Is a DNS Problem

The error has changed completely. You are now past TLS:

```
failed to lookup address information: Try again
```

That is `EAI_AGAIN` from `getaddrinfo(3)` — a DNS resolver failure, not a certificate problem.

## Why It Happens in Termux

Android does not expose `/etc/resolv.conf` in a form glibc/musl-style resolvers can read. On a normal Termux app the resolver still works because Bionic (Android's libc) calls into the system's `netd` service. But your binary is a **statically-linked musl build from CI**, so:

1. It uses musl's own resolver, not Bionic's.
2. Musl looks at `/etc/resolv.conf`, which on Android is empty/missing.
3. DNS lookups fail with `EAI_AGAIN`.

This is a well-known incompatibility between musl-static binaries and Android.

---

## Fixes (in order of preference)

### 1. Use the GNU build on Termux

Your release workflow already produces `linux-amd64-gnu`. Termux on aarch64 won't run x86-64 binaries directly, but if you are on `proot-distro` Ubuntu it will. For native Termux, add an aarch64-gnu target — but that requires cross-compilation against Android's libc, which is awkward.

### 2. Create a minimal `/etc/resolv.conf` for the musl binary

Termux does not own `/etc/`, but musl's resolver also honors the file at the path baked in at build time. The easiest workaround is to run the binary inside `proot` and bind-mount a resolv.conf:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/etc
cat > ~/etc/resolv.conf <<EOF
nameserver 1.1.1.1
nameserver 8.8.8.8
EOF

proot -b ~/etc/resolv.conf:/etc/resolv.conf gvm status
```

Or use Termux's `proot-distro`:

```bash
pkg install proot-distro
proot-distro install ubuntu
proot-distro login ubuntu
# inside the distro, /etc/resolv.conf works normally
```

### 3. Build for `aarch64-linux-android` (the proper fix)

Add an Android target to your release matrix so the binary uses Bionic and "just works" in Termux:

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yaml
- target: aarch64-linux-android
  os: ubuntu-latest
  cross: true
  archive_suffix: android-arm64
```

`cross` already supports `aarch64-linux-android` out of the box. The resulting binary links against Bionic via NDK and will resolve DNS through Android's normal mechanism — no `proot`, no manual `resolv.conf`.

### 4. Bypass the system resolver entirely

Switch reqwest to `hickory-dns`, a pure-Rust resolver that does its own UDP queries to nameservers you specify (or default public ones):

```toml
reqwest = { version = "0.13", features = ["json", "rustls", "hickory-dns"] }
```

You may still need to point it at servers explicitly because hickory also reads `/etc/resolv.conf` by default. But once configured, it works without any libc resolver at all.

---

## Recommended Path

For a tool meant to be used in Termux, **option 3** (an `aarch64-linux-android` artifact in your release workflow) is the only solution that produces a binary users can `curl | tar` and run directly. The musl artifact will keep tripping over Android-specific quirks like this one.

```.github/workflows/ci.yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: ["main"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["main"]

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  # ── Format ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  fmt:
    name: Rustfmt
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with rustfmt
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: rustfmt

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

  # ── Clippy ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  clippy:
    name: Clippy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with clippy
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-clippy-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-clippy-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Run Clippy (deny warnings)
        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

  # ── Test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test:
    name: Test suite
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-test-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-test-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --all-targets

      - name: Run tests
        run: cargo test --all-targets

  # ── MSRV ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  msrv:
    name: Minimum Supported Rust Version (1.95)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install MSRV toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@1.95.0

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-msrv-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-msrv-

      - name: Check compilation
        run: cargo check --all-targets

  # ── Audit ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  audit:
    name: Security audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install cargo-audit
        run: cargo install cargo-audit --locked

      - name: Run audit
        run: cargo audit

```

```.github/workflows/release.yaml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+"

permissions:
  contents: write

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
  BINARY_NAME: gvm

jobs:
  # ── Validate the tag matches Cargo.toml version ─────────────────────────────
  validate:
    name: Validate version
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Extract Cargo.toml version
        id: cargo_version
        run: |
          VERSION=$(grep '^version' Cargo.toml | head -1 | sed 's/.*= *"\(.*\)"/\1/')
          echo "version=v${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Compare tag vs Cargo.toml
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          CARGO="${{ steps.cargo_version.outputs.version }}"
          if [[ "$TAG" != "$CARGO" ]]; then
            echo "::error::Git tag ($TAG) does not match Cargo.toml version ($CARGO)"
            exit 1
          fi

  # ── Build static binaries ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  build:
    name: Build — ${{ matrix.target }}
    needs: validate
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          # Static x86-64 (musl)
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            cross: false
            archive_suffix: linux-amd64

          # Static ARM64 (musl, cross-compiled)
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            cross: true
            archive_suffix: linux-arm64

          # GNU x86-64 (wider glibc compatibility)
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-latest
            cross: false
            archive_suffix: linux-amd64-gnu

          # Windows x86-64 (MSVC)
          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            os: windows-latest
            cross: false
            archive_suffix: windows-amd64

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      # ── Toolchain ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      # ── musl libc for native builds ──────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install musl-tools (x86_64-musl)
        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools ca-certificates

      # ── Cross for cross-compiled targets ─────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install cross
        if: matrix.cross == true
        run: cargo install cross --locked

      # ── Cache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-release-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-release-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      # ── Compile ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Build (cargo)
        if: matrix.cross == false
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Build (cross)
        if: matrix.cross == true
        run: cross build --release --locked --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      # ── Package ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # Uses bash on all platforms (Git Bash on Windows).
      # Windows  → .zip  via 7z  (pre-installed on windows-latest)
      # Linux    → .tar.gz via tar
      - name: Create release archive
        id: package
        shell: bash
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"

          if [[ "$RUNNER_OS" == "Windows" ]]; then
            BINARY="target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}.exe"
            ARCHIVE="${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${TAG}-${{ matrix.archive_suffix }}.zip"
            mkdir staging
            cp "$BINARY" staging/
            cp README.md staging/
            7z a "$ARCHIVE" ./staging/*
          else
            BINARY="target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}"
            ARCHIVE="${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${TAG}-${{ matrix.archive_suffix }}.tar.gz"
            mkdir -p staging
            cp "$BINARY" staging/
            cp README.md staging/
            tar -czf "$ARCHIVE" -C staging .
          fi

          rm -rf staging
          echo "archive=${ARCHIVE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Generate SHA-256 checksum
        shell: bash
        run: |
          sha256sum "${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}" \
            > "${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}.sha256"

      # ── Upload artefacts for the next job ────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Upload artefacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: release-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: |
            ${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}
            ${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}.sha256
          retention-days: 1

  # ── Publish to crates.io ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  publish:
    name: Publish to crates.io
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: crates-io
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Publish
        env:
          CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
        run: cargo publish --locked

  # ── Create GitHub Release ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  github-release:
    name: Create GitHub Release
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Download all release artefacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          pattern: release-*
          merge-multiple: true
          path: dist/

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: notes
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          cat <<EOF > release-notes.md
          ### Installation

          #### via \`cargo install\`
          \`\`\`bash
          cargo install gvm --locked
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Linux)
          \`\`\`bash
          # x86-64 (static, musl)
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          sudo mv gvm /usr/local/bin/

          # ARM64 (static, musl)
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-linux-arm64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          sudo mv gvm /usr/local/bin/
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Windows)
          \`\`\`powershell
          # x86-64
          Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-windows-amd64.zip -OutFile gvm.zip
          Expand-Archive gvm.zip -DestinationPath .
          \`\`\`

          ### Checksums
          \`\`\`
          $(cat dist/*.sha256)
          \`\`\`
          EOF

      - name: Publish GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
        with:
          name: "gvm ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          body_path: release-notes.md
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

```

Implement #3 on both files

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: ["main"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["main"]

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  # ── Format ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  fmt:
    name: Rustfmt
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with rustfmt
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: rustfmt

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

  # ── Clippy ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  clippy:
    name: Clippy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with clippy
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-clippy-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-clippy-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Run Clippy (deny warnings)
        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

  # ── Test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test:
    name: Test suite
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-test-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-test-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --all-targets

      - name: Run tests
        run: cargo test --all-targets

  # ── Android target ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  android-build:
    name: Android (aarch64) build check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with Android target
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: aarch64-linux-android

      - name: Install cross
        run: cargo install cross --locked

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-android-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-android-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Build for Android
        run: cross build --target aarch64-linux-android

  # ── MSRV ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  msrv:
    name: Minimum Supported Rust Version (1.95)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install MSRV toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@1.95.0

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-msrv-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-msrv-

      - name: Check compilation
        run: cargo check --all-targets

  # ── Audit ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  audit:
    name: Security audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install cargo-audit
        run: cargo install cargo-audit --locked

      - name: Run audit
        run: cargo audit
```

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yaml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+"

permissions:
  contents: write

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
  BINARY_NAME: gvm

jobs:
  # ── Validate the tag matches Cargo.toml version ─────────────────────────────
  validate:
    name: Validate version
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Extract Cargo.toml version
        id: cargo_version
        run: |
          VERSION=$(grep '^version' Cargo.toml | head -1 | sed 's/.*= *"\(.*\)"/\1/')
          echo "version=v${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Compare tag vs Cargo.toml
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          CARGO="${{ steps.cargo_version.outputs.version }}"
          if [[ "$TAG" != "$CARGO" ]]; then
            echo "::error::Git tag ($TAG) does not match Cargo.toml version ($CARGO)"
            exit 1
          fi

  # ── Build static binaries ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  build:
    name: Build — ${{ matrix.target }}
    needs: validate
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          # Static x86-64 (musl)
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            cross: false
            archive_suffix: linux-amd64

          # Static ARM64 (musl, cross-compiled)
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            cross: true
            archive_suffix: linux-arm64

          # GNU x86-64 (wider glibc compatibility)
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-latest
            cross: false
            archive_suffix: linux-amd64-gnu

          # Android ARM64 (Bionic libc)
          - target: aarch64-linux-android
            os: ubuntu-latest
            cross: true
            archive_suffix: android-arm64

          # Windows x86-64 (MSVC)
          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            os: windows-latest
            cross: false
            archive_suffix: windows-amd64

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      # ── Toolchain ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      # ── musl libc for native builds ──────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install musl-tools (x86_64-musl)
        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools ca-certificates

      # ── Cross for cross-compiled targets ─────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install cross
        if: matrix.cross == true
        run: cargo install cross --locked

      # ── Cache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-release-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-release-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      # ── Compile ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Build (cargo)
        if: matrix.cross == false
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Build (cross)
        if: matrix.cross == true
        run: cross build --release --locked --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      # ── Package ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # Uses bash on all platforms (Git Bash on Windows).
      # Windows  → .zip  via 7z  (pre-installed on windows-latest)
      # Linux    → .tar.gz via tar
      - name: Create release archive
        id: package
        shell: bash
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"

          if [[ "$RUNNER_OS" == "Windows" ]]; then
            BINARY="target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}.exe"
            ARCHIVE="${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${TAG}-${{ matrix.archive_suffix }}.zip"
            mkdir staging
            cp "$BINARY" staging/
            cp README.md staging/
            7z a "$ARCHIVE" ./staging/*
          else
            BINARY="target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}"
            ARCHIVE="${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${TAG}-${{ matrix.archive_suffix }}.tar.gz"
            mkdir -p staging
            cp "$BINARY" staging/
            cp README.md staging/
            tar -czf "$ARCHIVE" -C staging .
          fi

          rm -rf staging
          echo "archive=${ARCHIVE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Generate SHA-256 checksum
        shell: bash
        run: |
          sha256sum "${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}" \
            > "${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}.sha256"

      # ── Upload artefacts for the next job ────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Upload artefacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: release-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: |
            ${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}
            ${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}.sha256
          retention-days: 1

  # ── Publish to crates.io ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  publish:
    name: Publish to crates.io
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: crates-io
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Publish
        env:
          CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
        run: cargo publish --locked

  # ── Create GitHub Release ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  github-release:
    name: Create GitHub Release
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Download all release artefacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          pattern: release-*
          merge-multiple: true
          path: dist/

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: notes
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          cat <<EOF > release-notes.md
          ### Installation

          #### via \`cargo install\`
          \`\`\`bash
          cargo install gvm --locked
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Linux)
          \`\`\`bash
          # x86-64 (static, musl)
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          sudo mv gvm /usr/local/bin/

          # ARM64 (static, musl)
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-linux-arm64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          sudo mv gvm /usr/local/bin/
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Android via Termux)
          \`\`\`bash
          # ARM64
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-android-arm64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          mv gvm \$PREFIX/bin/
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Windows)
          \`\`\`powershell
          # x86-64
          Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-windows-amd64.zip -OutFile gvm.zip
          Expand-Archive gvm.zip -DestinationPath .
          \`\`\`

          ### Checksums
          \`\`\`
          $(cat dist/*.sha256)
          \`\`\`
          EOF

      - name: Publish GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
        with:
          name: "gvm ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          body_path: release-notes.md
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true
```

## Changes Made

### ci.yaml
- Added new `android-build` job that:
  - Installs the `aarch64-linux-android` target
  - Installs `cross`
  - Builds the binary for Android to verify compilation works

### release.yaml
- Added `aarch64-linux-android` to the build matrix
- Updated release notes to include Android/Termux installation instructions with `$PREFIX` (the Termux environment variable for the app's prefix)

Now when you tag a release, an Android-compatible binary will be built alongside the Linux and Windows variants. Users on Termux can download and run it directly without DNS resolver issues.

Error: Process completed with exit code 101.

## Root Cause

`cross` uses an old Docker image with NDK r14b where `libunwind` does not exist as a standalone linkable library. The fix is to replace `cross` with `cargo-ndk` for the Android target, which uses a proper modern NDK.

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: ["main"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["main"]

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  # ── Format ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  fmt:
    name: Rustfmt
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with rustfmt
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: rustfmt

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

  # ── Clippy ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  clippy:
    name: Clippy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with clippy
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-clippy-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-clippy-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Run Clippy (deny warnings)
        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

  # ── Test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test:
    name: Test suite
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-test-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-test-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Build
        run: cargo build --all-targets

      - name: Run tests
        run: cargo test --all-targets

  # ── Android target ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  android-build:
    name: Android (aarch64) build check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain with Android target
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: aarch64-linux-android

      - name: Setup Android NDK
        uses: nttld/setup-ndk@v1
        with:
          ndk-version: r26d

      - name: Install cargo-ndk
        run: cargo install cargo-ndk --locked

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-android-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-android-
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-

      - name: Build for Android
        run: cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a build

  # ── MSRV ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  msrv:
    name: Minimum Supported Rust Version (1.95)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install MSRV toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@1.95.0

      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-msrv-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-msrv-

      - name: Check compilation
        run: cargo check --all-targets

  # ── Audit ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  audit:
    name: Security audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install cargo-audit
        run: cargo install cargo-audit --locked

      - name: Run audit
        run: cargo audit
```

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yaml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+"

permissions:
  contents: write

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
  BINARY_NAME: gvm

jobs:
  # ── Validate the tag matches Cargo.toml version ─────────────────────────────
  validate:
    name: Validate version
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Extract Cargo.toml version
        id: cargo_version
        run: |
          VERSION=$(grep '^version' Cargo.toml | head -1 | sed 's/.*= *"\(.*\)"/\1/')
          echo "version=v${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Compare tag vs Cargo.toml
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          CARGO="${{ steps.cargo_version.outputs.version }}"
          if [[ "$TAG" != "$CARGO" ]]; then
            echo "::error::Git tag ($TAG) does not match Cargo.toml version ($CARGO)"
            exit 1
          fi

  # ── Build static binaries ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  build:
    name: Build — ${{ matrix.target }}
    needs: validate
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          # Static x86-64 (musl)
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            method: cargo
            archive_suffix: linux-amd64

          # Static ARM64 (musl, cross-compiled)
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-musl
            os: ubuntu-latest
            method: cross
            archive_suffix: linux-arm64

          # GNU x86-64 (wider glibc compatibility)
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-latest
            method: cargo
            archive_suffix: linux-amd64-gnu

          # Android ARM64 (Bionic libc, Termux)
          - target: aarch64-linux-android
            os: ubuntu-latest
            method: ndk
            archive_suffix: android-arm64

          # Windows x86-64 (MSVC)
          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            os: windows-latest
            method: cargo
            archive_suffix: windows-amd64

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      # ── Toolchain ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      # ── musl libc for native x86-64 builds ──────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install musl-tools (x86_64-musl)
        if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-musl'
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools ca-certificates

      # ── cross for musl cross-compiled targets ────────────────────────────────
      - name: Install cross
        if: matrix.method == 'cross'
        run: cargo install cross --locked

      # ── NDK + cargo-ndk for Android ──────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Setup Android NDK
        if: matrix.method == 'ndk'
        uses: nttld/setup-ndk@v1
        with:
          ndk-version: r26d

      - name: Install cargo-ndk
        if: matrix.method == 'ndk'
        run: cargo install cargo-ndk --locked

      # ── Cache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Cache cargo registry & build artefacts
        uses: actions/cache@v5
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-release-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-release-
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      # ── Compile ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Build (cargo)
        if: matrix.method == 'cargo'
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Build (cross)
        if: matrix.method == 'cross'
        run: cross build --release --locked --target ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Build (cargo-ndk)
        if: matrix.method == 'ndk'
        run: cargo ndk -t arm64-v8a build --release --locked

      # ── Package ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Create release archive
        id: package
        shell: bash
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"

          if [[ "$RUNNER_OS" == "Windows" ]]; then
            BINARY="target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}.exe"
            ARCHIVE="${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${TAG}-${{ matrix.archive_suffix }}.zip"
            mkdir staging
            cp "$BINARY" staging/
            cp README.md staging/
            7z a "$ARCHIVE" ./staging/*
          else
            BINARY="target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}"
            ARCHIVE="${{ env.BINARY_NAME }}-${TAG}-${{ matrix.archive_suffix }}.tar.gz"
            mkdir -p staging
            cp "$BINARY" staging/
            cp README.md staging/
            tar -czf "$ARCHIVE" -C staging .
          fi

          rm -rf staging
          echo "archive=${ARCHIVE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Generate SHA-256 checksum
        shell: bash
        run: |
          sha256sum "${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}" \
            > "${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}.sha256"

      # ── Upload artefacts for the next job ────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Upload artefacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: release-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: |
            ${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}
            ${{ steps.package.outputs.archive }}.sha256
          retention-days: 1

  # ── Publish to crates.io ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  publish:
    name: Publish to crates.io
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: crates-io
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install stable toolchain
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Publish
        env:
          CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
        run: cargo publish --locked

  # ── Create GitHub Release ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  github-release:
    name: Create GitHub Release
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Download all release artefacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          pattern: release-*
          merge-multiple: true
          path: dist/

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: notes
        run: |
          TAG="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          cat <<EOF > release-notes.md
          ### Installation

          #### via \`cargo install\`
          \`\`\`bash
          cargo install gvm --locked
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Linux)
          \`\`\`bash
          # x86-64 (static, musl)
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          sudo mv gvm /usr/local/bin/

          # ARM64 (static, musl)
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-linux-arm64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          sudo mv gvm /usr/local/bin/
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Android via Termux)
          \`\`\`bash
          # ARM64
          curl -fsSL https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-android-arm64.tar.gz | tar -xz
          mv gvm \$PREFIX/bin/
          \`\`\`

          #### Pre-built binary (Windows)
          \`\`\`powershell
          # x86-64
          Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${TAG}/gvm-${TAG}-windows-amd64.zip -OutFile gvm.zip
          Expand-Archive gvm.zip -DestinationPath .
          \`\`\`

          ### Checksums
          \`\`\`
          $(cat dist/*.sha256)
          \`\`\`
          EOF

      - name: Publish GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
        with:
          name: "gvm ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          body_path: release-notes.md
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true
```

## Summary of Changes

### Both files — Android build tooling
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `cross: true` + cross Docker image (NDK r14b) | `method: ndk` + `nttld/setup-ndk@v1` (NDK r26d) + `cargo-ndk` |

### `release.yaml` — matrix refactor
- Replaced the `cross: bool` flag with a three-value `method: cargo \| cross \| ndk` field — cleaner and avoids boolean edge cases in `if:` conditions
- Added `setup-ndk` and `cargo-ndk` installation steps gated on `method == 'ndk'`
- Build step now has three branches matching the three methods

### Why NDK r26d
It is the latest LTS release; ships LLVM 17 with a complete `libunwind` integrated into the compiler runtime rather than as a separate `-lunwind` link target, which is exactly what was failing.
